dependencies.relationship: Modifies integration tests first written in 01_basic_svc group 1

subtasks.title: Add TestTS02_1, TestTS02_2, TestTS02_3 to audit_events_test.go
details: Create `internal/integration/audit_events_test.go` (note: `validation_test.go` already exists with spec 01 edge-case tests; use a new file to avoid conflicts).
TestTS02_1: POST canonical valid AuditEvent JSON, assert HTTP 201 with zero-byte body and no Content-Type header, query DB and verify all 9 event columns match; parse received_at as time.RFC3339Nano.
TestTS02_2: POST three invalid payloads as separate requests — (a) missing event_type, (b) payload as array, (c) empty id — assert HTTP 201 and zero rows in events table for each.
TestTS02_3: POST canonical valid AuditEvent twice with the same id, assert HTTP 201 both times, verify exactly one row in events table.

Use canonical AuditEvent fixture: id='7c10ec9b-daaf-4146-8e40-6efc92e5db39', timestamp='2026-06-18T11:26:26.527713+00:00', run_id='20260618_112626_17dd3f', event_type='run.start', node_id='', session_id='', archetype='', severity='info', payload={"plan_hash":"abc123"}.

subtasks.title: Update existing TestTS01_1, TestTS01_2, TestTS01_5 to use canonical AuditEvent payload
details: Modify `internal/integration/events_test.go`.
Replace arbitrary JSON payloads in TestTS01_1, TestTS01_2, and TestTS01_5 with the canonical valid AuditEvent JSON body.
Ensure each test uses a distinct `id` value to avoid primary key conflicts across test runs.
All other assertions in those tests remain unchanged.

subtasks.title: Write unit tests for AuditEvent struct, InsertEvent signature, and events table schema
details: Update `internal/db/db_test.go` to verify: PRAGMA table_info(events) returns exactly 10 columns with correct names, types, and constraints (id TEXT PRIMARY KEY, timestamp/run_id/event_type/node_id/session_id/archetype/severity/payload TEXT NOT NULL, received_at DATETIME NOT NULL).
Add test for InsertEvent signature: func InsertEvent(ctx context.Context, db *sql.DB, event AuditEvent) error in package db.
Add test that calls InsertEvent with a valid AuditEvent, checks no error returned, and verifies the row in DB including received_at parseable as time.RFC3339Nano.
Add test that unmarshals canonical JSON into AuditEvent and asserts all 9 fields have correct Go types and JSON tags, with Payload being json.RawMessage.
Add test verifying CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS DDL creates the table on a fresh database (TS-02-13).

subtasks.title: Write edge-case and property-style integration tests
details: Add integration test cases covering: malformed JSON body (TS-02-E2), top-level JSON array (TS-02-E3), JSON null body (TS-02-E4), empty id string (TS-02-E5), empty severity string (TS-02-E6), array payload (TS-02-E7), null payload (TS-02-E8).
Add test for empty body returning HTTP 400 (TS-02-E9) — confirm existing behavior is preserved.
Add unit test for DDL failure path: init_db on a failing DB handle should call log.Fatal / os.Exit with non-zero code (TS-02-E10).
Add unit test for old 3-column schema: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS is a no-op and startup proceeds without error (TS-02-E11).
These tests may be added to `internal/integration/validation_test.go` or a separate `internal/db/db_test.go` as appropriate.

subtasks.title: Update helpers_test.go to match the new 10-column events table schema
details: Modify `internal/integration/helpers_test.go` to reflect the new 10-column schema.
Update any helper that reads from or queries the events table to include all 10 columns: id, timestamp, run_id, event_type, node_id, session_id, archetype, severity, payload, received_at.
Remove any references to the old three-column schema (id, payload, received_at only).
Ensure test helpers correctly handle scanning all 10 columns from query results.

checks: All new and updated test files compile without errors: `go build ./...`
go vet reports no issues: `go vet ./...`
New test functions (TestTS02_1, TestTS02_2, TestTS02_3) exist and are discoverable: `go test ./internal/integration/... -list 'TestTS02.*'`
Tests that depend on unimplemented production code fail with compilation or stub errors rather than panicking unexpectedly — confirm tests are structurally correct
02-REQ-9.1, 02-REQ-9.2, 02-REQ-9.3, 02-REQ-9.4 acceptance criteria are represented by test cases

task_groups.title: Define AuditEvent struct and update InsertEvent in db package

subtasks.title: Add AuditEvent struct to internal/db/events.go
details: In `internal/db/events.go`, define the AuditEvent struct with exactly 9 exported fields and JSON struct tags:
ID        string          `json:"id"`
Timestamp string          `json:"timestamp"`
RunID     string          `json:"run_id"`
EventType string          `json:"event_type"`
NodeID    string          `json:"node_id"`
SessionID string          `json:"session_id"`
Archetype string          `json:"archetype"`
Severity  string          `json:"severity"`
Payload   json.RawMessage `json:"payload"`
Import `encoding/json` in the db package.
Do NOT use `json.Decoder` with `DisallowUnknownFields` anywhere — Go default json.Unmarshal behavior (ignoring unknown fields) is required.

subtasks.title: Update InsertEvent function signature and implementation
details: Update the signature of InsertEvent in `internal/db/events.go` to: `func InsertEvent(ctx context.Context, db *sql.DB, event AuditEvent) error`
Implementation must execute: `INSERT OR IGNORE INTO events (id, timestamp, run_id, event_type, node_id, session_id, archetype, severity, payload, received_at) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)`
Use `db.ExecContext(ctx, query, ...)` — no explicit transaction wrapper.
Serialize the payload field via `json.Marshal(event.Payload)` before storing; convert result to string.
Generate `received_at` as `time.Now().UTC().Format(time.RFC3339Nano)`.
Return nil if INSERT OR IGNORE succeeds (including duplicate id case); return non-nil error only for genuine INSERT failures.
Remove any server-side UUID generation that was previously part of InsertEvent.

subtasks.title: Update all existing call sites of InsertEvent to pass AuditEvent struct
details: Search the codebase for all existing calls to InsertEvent and update them to pass a db.AuditEvent struct instead of flat positional arguments.
Confirm that `internal/handler/events.go` references h.DB (the existing *sql.DB field on EventsHandler) when calling InsertEvent — no new injection mechanism.
Ensure the `internal/handler` package imports `internal/db` and uses `db.AuditEvent` directly without any separate DTO.

checks: Unit tests for AuditEvent struct and InsertEvent pass: `go test ./internal/db/... -count=1 -v`
go vet reports no issues: `go vet ./internal/db/...`
AuditEvent struct has exactly 9 fields with correct types and JSON tags — verified by TS-02-1 and TS-02-2 passing
InsertEvent uses INSERT OR IGNORE with all 9 event fields plus received_at — verified by TS-02-17 passing
02-REQ-1.1, 02-REQ-1.2, 02-REQ-7.1, 02-REQ-7.2, 02-REQ-6.1 acceptance criteria met

task_groups.title: Update events table DDL in db.go

subtasks.title: Replace schema DDL in internal/db/db.go with new 10-column CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS
details: In `internal/db/db.go`, replace the existing schema DDL with the authoritative 10-column DDL exactly as specified:

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS events (id TEXT PRIMARY KEY, timestamp TEXT NOT NULL, run_id TEXT NOT NULL, event_type TEXT NOT NULL, node_id TEXT NOT NULL, session_id TEXT NOT NULL, archetype TEXT NOT NULL, severity TEXT NOT NULL, payload TEXT NOT NULL, received_at DATETIME NOT NULL);
Do NOT include any DROP TABLE statement anywhere in db.go.
Do NOT add WITHOUT ROWID, STRICT, collation, or index options.
Use `id` from the event as the primary key — remove any server-side UUID generation for the primary key.

subtasks.title: Add fatal-exit behaviour if CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS DDL fails
details: After executing the CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS statement, check the returned error.
If the error is non-nil, call log.Fatal (or equivalent) with a descriptive message referencing the DDL failure; this causes the process to exit with a non-zero exit code.
The service must never continue running without a valid events table.
This matches the existing startup failure behavior of the codebase.

subtasks.title: Verify CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS is a no-op on pre-existing table
details: Confirm by code inspection and unit test that IF NOT EXISTS semantics mean startup proceeds normally when the events table already exists (even with old 3-column schema).
No migration logic or DROP TABLE is attempted — the DDL statement simply produces no error.
Unit test (TS-02-E11): open a SQLite DB with old 3-column schema, call init_db, assert no fatal exit and no error returned.

checks: Unit tests for DB initialization pass: `go test ./internal/db/... -count=1 -v -run 'TestTS02'`
PRAGMA table_info(events) returns exactly 10 columns — verified by TS-02-9 and TS-02-13 passing
No DROP TABLE found anywhere in internal/db/db.go — verified by TS-02-14 passing
DDL failure causes log.Fatal / non-zero exit — verified by TS-02-E10 passing
go vet reports no issues: `go vet ./internal/db/...`
02-REQ-4.1, 02-REQ-5.1, 02-REQ-5.2, 02-REQ-5.E1 acceptance criteria met

task_groups.title: Implement validation logic in the POST /v1/events handler

subtasks.title: Replace raw JSON passthrough with single-pass json.Unmarshal into AuditEvent
details: In `internal/handler/events.go`, replace the existing raw JSON passthrough with `json.Unmarshal(body, &event)` where event is of type `db.AuditEvent`.
Read the request body bytes first (e.g. via io.ReadAll or c.Request().Body).

If json.Unmarshal returns any error (malformed JSON, top-level array, scalar, null), immediately call `c.NoContent(http.StatusCreated)` and return nil — do NOT store the event and do NOT log an error.
Do NOT use json.Decoder with DisallowUnknownFields — unknown fields are silently ignored per Go default behavior.
Do NOT perform a two-pass unmarshal.

subtasks.title: Add non-empty string validation for required fields
details: After successful json.Unmarshal, check that the following fields are non-empty strings: event.ID, event.Timestamp, event.RunID, event.EventType, event.Severity.
If any of these fields is an empty string, call `c.NoContent(http.StatusCreated)` and return nil — do NOT store and do NOT log an error.
Do NOT reject the event if event.NodeID, event.SessionID, or event.Archetype are empty strings — these are valid.

subtasks.title: Add payload first-byte check for object type
details: After non-empty string checks pass, inspect event.Payload (json.RawMessage).
Trim leading whitespace from the raw bytes using `bytes.TrimLeft(event.Payload, " \t\r\n")` or equivalent.
If the trimmed slice is empty or the first byte is not `{`, call `c.NoContent(http.StatusCreated)` and return nil — do NOT store and do NOT log an error.
This check covers: array payload (first byte '['), null payload (first byte 'n'), string payload (first byte '"'), numeric payload (digit), and empty payload field.

subtasks.title: Call InsertEvent on successfully validated event and return 201
details: After all validation passes, call `db.InsertEvent(c.Request().Context(), h.DB, event)`.
Access the database handle via h.DB — the existing EventsHandler struct field; no new injection mechanism.
If InsertEvent returns a non-nil error (not a duplicate key — those return nil), propagate the error appropriately (return an HTTP 500 or use Echo's error handling).
For all successful paths (including duplicate id ignored by INSERT OR IGNORE), return `c.NoContent(http.StatusCreated)`.
The response for ALL non-empty request bodies (valid or invalid) is HTTP 201 with zero-byte body and no Content-Type header.

checks: Integration tests for handler validation pass: `go test ./internal/integration/... -count=1 -v -run 'TestTS02'`
go vet reports no issues: `go vet ./internal/handler/...`
TS-02-4 (valid event stored), TS-02-6 (array payload rejected), TS-02-E2 (malformed JSON rejected), TS-02-E5 (empty id rejected), TS-02-E9 (empty body → 400) all pass
TS-02-7 (extra fields accepted) and TS-02-E1 pass — unknown fields are silently ignored
02-REQ-2.1, 02-REQ-2.2, 02-REQ-2.3, 02-REQ-2.4, 02-REQ-3.1 acceptance criteria met

task_groups.title: Integration checkpoint — all spec tests pass end-to-end

subtasks.title: Run full integration test suite including updated TestTS01 tests
details: Run the full integration test suite: `go test ./internal/integration/... -count=1 -v`
Confirm TestTS02_1, TestTS02_2, TestTS02_3 all pass.
Confirm TestTS01_1, TestTS01_2, TestTS01_5 pass with the updated canonical AuditEvent payloads.
Confirm existing auth/health-check tests (401, 400 for empty body, GET /healthz, GET /readyz) still pass — no regressions in 02-REQ-8.1.

subtasks.title: Run full unit test suite and linter
details: Run: `go test ./... -count=1` — all tests (unit + integration) must pass.
Run: `go vet ./...` — no issues reported.
Confirm that PRAGMA table_info(events) returns 10 columns in the db unit tests.
Confirm InsertEvent signature unit test passes.

subtasks.title: Verify silent rejection indistinguishability property
details: Confirm via TS-02-8 and TS-02-P1 scenarios that both valid and invalid (non-empty) POST /v1/events requests return HTTP 201 with zero-byte body and no Content-Type header.
Manually verify with curl or test that no Content-Type header leaks into 201 responses.
Confirm 02-PROP-1 (silent rejection indistinguishability) holds across the covered test cases.

subtasks.title: Verify no storage of invalid events property
details: Confirm that after all invalid-payload tests run, the events table contains zero rows for those submissions.
Verify 02-PROP-2 (no storage of invalid events) holds: malformed JSON, missing field, empty required string, non-object payload — none result in a DB row.
Check DB row count assertions in TestTS02_2, TS-02-E2 through TS-02-E8.

checks: All spec tests pass: `go test ./internal/integration/... -count=1 -v -run 'TestTS02'`
All existing spec 01 integration tests still pass: `go test ./internal/integration/... -count=1 -v -run 'TestTS01'`
Full test suite passes with no failures: `go test ./... -count=1`
go vet reports no issues: `go vet ./...`
02-REQ-9.1, 02-REQ-9.2, 02-REQ-9.3, 02-REQ-9.4, 02-REQ-8.1 acceptance criteria all met

task_groups.title: Wiring verification — end-to-end integration and stub audit

subtasks.title: Trace execution paths — verify function call chains have no stubs
details: 02-PATH-1 (valid event): Confirm handler reads body → json.Unmarshal into db.AuditEvent → non-empty checks → payload byte check → db.InsertEvent(ctx, h.DB, event) → c.NoContent(201). No stub or panic("not implemented") in any step.
02-PATH-2 (invalid event): Confirm each validation failure path calls c.NoContent(201) immediately and does NOT call InsertEvent. No unreachable code.
02-PATH-3 (duplicate): Confirm INSERT OR IGNORE in InsertEvent returns nil on conflict; handler calls c.NoContent(201).
02-PATH-4 (startup DDL success): Confirm service startup executes CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS and proceeds to serve requests.
02-PATH-5 (startup DDL failure): Confirm log.Fatal is called and process exits — no HTTP server started.
Search codebase for stub markers: `grep -rn 'panic("not implemented")\|// TODO\|panic("TODO")' internal/` — must return no results in production code paths.

subtasks.title: Verify return value propagation is correct throughout the call chain
details: Confirm that the error return from InsertEvent is received and handled by the handler — not silently discarded.
Confirm nil error from InsertEvent (including INSERT OR IGNORE no-op) results in c.NoContent(201).
Confirm non-nil error from InsertEvent (genuine DB failure) is propagated appropriately (e.g. returned to Echo's error handler).
Confirm json.Unmarshal error return is checked before proceeding to validation steps.
Confirm bytes.TrimLeft result is used for the first-byte check and the result of that check is respected.

subtasks.title: Run all smoke tests with real components
details: TS-02-SMOKE-1: POST canonical valid AuditEvent → HTTP 201, zero-byte body, no Content-Type, one row in events with all 9 event columns and valid received_at.
TS-02-SMOKE-2: POST invalid event (missing event_type) → HTTP 201, zero-byte body, no Content-Type, zero rows in events.
TS-02-SMOKE-3: POST canonical valid AuditEvent twice (same id) → HTTP 201 both times, exactly one row in events.
TS-02-SMOKE-4: Start service with fresh SQLite DB → CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS succeeds, events table has 10 columns, GET /healthz returns 200.
TS-02-SMOKE-5: Start service with failing DB handle → log.Fatal called, process exits non-zero, no HTTP server started.
All smoke tests must pass using real Echo server, real SQLite DB, and real InsertEvent — no mocks in smoke tests.

subtasks.title: Stub and dead-code audit across all modified files
details: Run: `grep -rn 'panic("not implemented")\|panic("TODO")\|// TODO\|// FIXME' internal/db/events.go internal/db/db.go internal/handler/events.go` — must return no results.
Confirm no bare `return nil` in InsertEvent that would skip the actual DB exec.
Confirm no bare `return nil` in the handler that would skip the full validation chain.
Confirm AuditEvent struct has all 9 fields with non-zero-value JSON tags — no placeholder or dummy field names.
Confirm the DDL string in db.go matches the authoritative 10-column schema exactly — no missing columns.

subtasks.title: Cross-spec entry point verification — spec 01 tests call updated handler
details: Confirm that TestTS01_1, TestTS01_2, TestTS01_5 in `internal/integration/events_test.go` now send the canonical valid AuditEvent JSON (all 9 fields, correct types) and not the old arbitrary JSON.
Confirm those tests call the same POST /v1/events endpoint and exercise the updated handler (with validation logic active).
Confirm no regression in authentication tests, Content-Type validation tests, health check tests (GET /healthz, GET /readyz).
Run: `go test ./internal/integration/... -count=1 -v` — all tests including TestTS01_* and TestTS02_* must pass.

checks: All smoke tests pass end-to-end with real components: `go test ./internal/integration/... -count=1 -v -run 'TestTS02'`
No stub markers found: `grep -rn 'panic(\"not implemented\")\|// TODO' internal/db/events.go internal/db/db.go internal/handler/events.go` returns empty
Full test suite passes: `go test ./... -count=1` — zero failures
go vet reports no issues: `go vet ./...`
All 5 execution paths (02-PATH-1 through 02-PATH-5) verified as fully wired with no stubs
TestTS01_1, TestTS01_2, TestTS01_5 pass with canonical AuditEvent payloads: `go test ./internal/integration/... -count=1 -v -run 'TestTS01'`
02-PROP-1, 02-PROP-2, 02-PROP-3, 02-PROP-4, 02-PROP-5, 02-PROP-6 correctness properties all verified by passing tests
